mainEntity.name: How long does subsidiary registration take?

acceptedAnswer.text: 15-30 days for WOS (Pvt Ltd route). 30-60 days for Branch/Liaison Office (requires RBI approval).
Yes. FC-GPR must be filed within 30 days of share allotment. FEMA valuation certificate required. Ongoing FLA return annually.
Investments from countries sharing a land border with India (China, Pakistan, etc.) require prior government approval regardless of sector.

mainEntity.name: What is a wholly owned subsidiary (WOS)?

acceptedAnswer.text: A company where 100% shares are held by a foreign parent company. Registered as Indian private limited company under Companies Act. Subject to FDI regulations for the relevant sector.

mainEntity.name: What are the steps to set up Indian subsidiary?

acceptedAnswer.text: Check FDI sectoral cap, reserve company name, obtain DSC/DIN for directors, file SPICe+ for incorporation, receive CoI, file FC-GPR for FDI reporting, open bank account, apply for GST/PF/ESI as needed.

mainEntity.name: Can a foreign company have 100% FDI in Indian subsidiary?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, in most sectors under automatic route. Exceptions: defence (74%), media (49%), insurance (74%), multi-brand retail (51%), with sector-specific conditions. Check consolidated FDI policy for your sector.

mainEntity.name: What compliance is required for Indian subsidiary?

acceptedAnswer.text: All Companies Act compliance plus: FC-GPR filing (30 days of share allotment), FLA return (July 15 annually), transfer pricing documentation if transactions with parent exceed ₹1 crore, and withholding tax on payments to parent.

mainEntity.name: Is branch office or subsidiary better for foreign companies?

acceptedAnswer.text: Subsidiary: separate legal entity, limited liability, easier to raise local funding, can operate independently. Branch: extension of parent, unlimited liability, limited activities (RBI approval needed), easier to repatriate profits. Subsidiary preferred for long-term operations.
